page.title: Passive Reporting Structures
page.subtitle_html: Passive Reporting Structures - C1 impersonal passive, personal passive, and perfect infinitive reporting patterns.
page.subtitle_text: C1 impersonal passive, personal passive, and perfect infinitive reporting patterns.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Passive Reporting Structures: compact C1 anchors for it is said that, he is said to, and perfect infinitive reporting structures.","rules":[{"label":"Impersonal passive","text":"Use it is said, believed, thought, reported, or claimed that + clause.","example":"It is believed that the plan will succeed."},{"label":"Personal passive","text":"Use subject + is or are said/believed/thought/reported + to + verb.","example":"The company is reported to employ 500 people."},{"label":"Perfect infinitive","text":"Use to have + past participle for a reported earlier action.","example":"He is said to have left the country."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English C1 lesson package.
